statement: Unauthorized remote access occurred via a RAT or backdoor installed on the ROCBA system
rationale: Case premise is unauthorized access; memory triage (process list, injected code, network connections) will confirm or disprove active C2 and suspicious processes

history.note: svchost.exe PID 1248 has dozens of ESTABLISHED and CLOSED connections to two non-Microsoft external IPs on high ephemeral ports within a 5-minute burst (2020-11-16 02:31–02:36 UTC). Pending: identify which service runs under PID 1248 and verify via disk artifacts.

SELF-CORRECTION: Previous update incorrectly classified svchost PID 1248 as C2. Re-examination shows all connections from 213.202.233.104 and 81.30.144.115 have local address 192.168.1.5:3389 — these are INBOUND RDP connections; PID 1248 is TermService. Those IPs are attack sources (brute-force RDP), not C2 servers. Unauthorized access IS confirmed via RDP credential abuse (Events 4778/4779 for fredr; LNK timestamps; ESTABLISHED RDP sessions at capture time from attack IPs). No outbound RAT/C2 confirmed in memory; Event 7045 service install (2020-11-11) remains a possible persistence mechanism but is no longer corroborated by active C2 beaconing.

statement: Sensitive data was exfiltrated — either via network (cloud/C2) or physical media (USB)
rationale: Unauthorized access cases typically involve data theft; browser history, cloud artifacts, USB history, and network connections will confirm or disprove

history.note: LNK files prove staged exfiltration: classified SRL research documents (Megaforce, Blue Thunder, Wolves Lair, ADAMANTIUM, RareEarthDeposits_Confidential) were browsed from F: drive (external USB, Toshiba connected 02:29:30 UTC). PST email archive exported to Google Drive (G:). BitLocker keys accessed. All activity Nov 13-14, 2020 via RDP.

statement: Anti-forensic techniques were employed to hide the intrusion (log wiping, timestomping, or secure deletion)
rationale: Sophisticated actors routinely clear logs or stomp timestamps; event log gaps, MFT anomalies, and prefetch records will test this

history.note: SDelete.zip accessed 2020-11-14 13:37 (T1070.004). Event 1102 confirmed (audit log cleared — T1070.001). Prefetch disabled (0 entries — may be Server/SSD or deliberate). Need to confirm SDelete execution via shimcache/amcache.

statement: The memory image is post-incident and the attacker process/connection is no longer resident; disk artifacts carry the primary evidence
rationale: If memory shows only clean system processes with no injected code or suspicious connections, the incident was prior to capture; disk artifacts will be the primary forensic record

history.note: Memory is NOT post-incident — svchost.exe PID 1248 has active C2 connections to 213.202.233.104 and 81.30.144.115 at the time of capture. Disk artifacts still needed to understand persistence and full attack chain.

UPDATED RATIONALE: Memory is NOT post-incident. At capture time (2020-11-16 02:31 UTC), ESTABLISHED RDP sessions from 213.202.233.104 and 81.30.144.115 to 192.168.1.5:3389 indicate the attacker was actively connected via RDP when memory was acquired — concurrent with ROCBA-SYSTEM.lnk creation at 02:31:29 UTC. Note: These are inbound RDP connections, not outbound C2 as previously stated.
